body:
Closed as salvaged — autonomous dispatch produced the required read-only audit
(5-question answer block for the W16 `rge-asset-store` integration shape) cleanly
in round 0, but the canonical verify gate caught an unrelated regression
(`STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION` from `rge-editor`'s test binary, traced to concurrent
`wgpu::GfxContext` lifecycle in 8 GPU-bearing tests, 5 of which were added by
recent dispatches #85 / #87 / #89).

The auto-routed CORRECTION packet correctly preserved the audit content but
asked the executor to fix the unrelated test crash, expanding scope beyond the
read-only intent. The Claude execute round 1 then hung on the runtime side
(`node` at 0.05s CPU over 29+ minutes); the queue parent process was manually
stopped.

**Salvage on main (separate commits):**

- `84f2b82` — `chore(infra): serialize GPU/readback tests in rge-editor with GPU_TEST_LOCK`
- `40ba8b0` — `chore(infra): add Watch-DispatchStages.ps1 standalone dispatch progress watcher`
- `27f6e1b` — `chore(dispatch): add scope-preserving halt clause to task #4`

The audit content from round 0 is preserved locally at
`OLD/audit-issue91-task4-readonly-preflight.md` (gitignored). The full
dispatch packet chain is preserved at `OLD/dispatch-issue91-salvage/`.

Task #4 will be re-run as a fresh autonomous dispatch under the new halt
clause, which routes unrelated verify failures to `NEEDS_HUMAN` instead of
accepting a scope-expanding CORRECTION. The new dispatch will produce a fresh
audit; the preserved round 0 audit content is a cross-reference for consistency
verification.
